children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of section 38, in the case of a taxpayer who holds a qualified equity investment on a credit allowance date of such investment which occurs during the taxable year, the new markets tax credit determined under this section for such taxable year is an amount equal to the applicable percentage of the amount paid to the qualified community development entity for such investment at its original issue.

children.label: A
children.text: 5 percent with respect to the first 3 credit allowance dates, and

children.label: B
children.text: 6 percent with respect to the remainder of the credit allowance dates.

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which such investment is initially made, and

children.label: B
children.text: each of the 6 anniversary dates of such date thereafter.

children.label: A
children.text: such investment is acquired by the taxpayer at its original issue (directly or through an underwriter) solely in exchange for cash,

children.label: B
children.text: substantially all of such cash is used by the qualified community development entity to make qualified low-income community investments, and

children.label: C
children.text: such investment is designated for purposes of this section by the qualified community development entity.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The maximum amount of equity investments issued by a qualified community development entity which may be designated under paragraph (1)(C) by such entity shall not exceed the portion of the limitation amount allocated under subsection (f) to such entity.

children.heading: Safe harbor for determining use of cash
children.label: 3 Safe harbor for determining use of cash
children.text: The requirement of paragraph (1)(B) shall be treated as met if at least 85 percent of the aggregate gross assets of the qualified community development entity are invested in qualified low-income community investments.

children.heading: Treatment of subsequent purchasers
children.label: 4 Treatment of subsequent purchasers
children.text: The term “qualified equity investment” includes any equity investment which would (but for paragraph (1)(A)) be a qualified equity investment in the hands of the taxpayer if such investment was a qualified equity investment in the hands of a prior holder.

children.heading: Redemptions
children.label: 5 Redemptions
children.text: A rule similar to the rule of section 1202(c)(3) shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: any stock (other than nonqualified preferred stock as defined in section 351(g)(2)) in an entity which is a corporation, and

children.label: B
children.text: any capital interest in an entity which is a partnership.

children.label: A
children.text: the primary mission of the entity is serving, or providing investment capital for, low-income communities or low-income persons,

children.label: B
children.text: the entity maintains accountability to residents of low-income communities through their representation on any governing board of the entity or on any advisory board to the entity, and

children.label: C
children.text: the entity is certified by the Secretary for purposes of this section as being a qualified community development entity.

children.label: A
children.text: any specialized small business investment company (as defined in section 1044(c)(3)), and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: any community development financial institution (as defined in section 103 of the Community Development Banking and Financial Institutions Act of 1994 ()).12 U.S.C. 4702

children.label: A
children.text: any capital or equity investment in, or loan to, any qualified active low-income community business,

children.label: B
children.text: the purchase from another qualified community development entity of any loan made by such entity which is a qualified low-income community investment,

children.label: C
children.text: financial counseling and other services specified in regulations prescribed by the Secretary to businesses located in, and residents of, low-income communities, and

children.label: D
children.text: any equity investment in, or loan to, any qualified community development entity.

children.label: i
children.text: at least 50 percent of the total gross income of such entity is derived from the active conduct of a qualified business within any low-income community,

children.label: ii
children.text: a substantial portion of the use of the tangible property of such entity (whether owned or leased) is within any low-income community,

children.label: iii
children.text: a substantial portion of the services performed for such entity by its employees are performed in any low-income community,

children.label: iv
children.text: less than 5 percent of the average of the aggregate unadjusted bases of the property of such entity is attributable to collectibles (as defined in section 408(m)(2)) other than collectibles that are held primarily for sale to customers in the ordinary course of such business, and

children.label: v
children.text: less than 5 percent of the average of the aggregate unadjusted bases of the property of such entity is attributable to nonqualified financial property (as defined in section 1397C(e)).

children.heading: Proprietorship
children.label: B Proprietorship
children.text: Such term shall include any business carried on by an individual as a proprietor if such business would meet the requirements of subparagraph (A) were it incorporated.

children.heading: Portions of business may be qualified active low-income community business
children.label: C Portions of business may be qualified active low-income community business
children.text: The term “qualified active low-income community business” includes any trades or businesses which would qualify as a qualified active low-income community business if such trades or businesses were separately incorporated.

children.label: A
children.text: in lieu of applying paragraph (2)(B) thereof, the rental to others of real property located in any low-income community shall be treated as a qualified business if there are substantial improvements located on such property, and

children.label: B
children.text: paragraph (3) thereof shall not apply.

children.label: A
children.text: the poverty rate for such tract is at least 20 percent, or

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a tract not located within a metropolitan area, the median family income for such tract does not exceed 80 percent of statewide median family income, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a tract located within a metropolitan area, the median family income for such tract does not exceed 80 percent of the greater of statewide median family income or the metropolitan area median family income.

children.heading: Targeted populations
children.label: 2 Targeted populations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe regulations under which 1 or more targeted populations (within the meaning of section 103(20) of the Riegle Community Development and Regulatory Improvement Act of 1994 ()) may be treated as low-income communities. Such regulations shall include procedures for determining which entities are qualified active low-income community businesses with respect to such populations.12 U.S.C. 4702(20)

children.heading: Areas not within census tracts
children.label: 3 Areas not within census tracts
children.text: In the case of an area which is not tracted for population census tracts, the equivalent county divisions (as defined by the Bureau of the Census for purposes of defining poverty areas) shall be used for purposes of determining poverty rates and median family income.

children.label: A
children.text: is within an empowerment zone the designation of which is in effect under section 1391, and

children.label: B
children.text: is contiguous to 1 or more low-income communities (determined without regard to this paragraph).

children.heading: Modification of income requirement for census tracts within high migration rural counties
children.label: 5 Modification of income requirement for census tracts within high migration rural counties

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a population census tract located within a high migration rural county, paragraph (1)(B)(i) shall be applied by substituting “85 percent” for “80 percent”.

children.heading: High migration rural county
children.label: B High migration rural county
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “high migration rural county” means any county which, during the 20-year period ending with the year in which the most recent census was conducted, has a net out-migration of inhabitants from the county of at least 10 percent of the population of the county at the beginning of such period.

children.heading: National limitation on amount of investments designated
children.label: f National limitation on amount of investments designated
G
children.text: $3,500,000,000 for each of calendar years 2010 through 2019, and

children.label: H
children.text: $5,000,000,000 for each calendar year after 2019.

children.label: A
children.text: with a record of having successfully provided capital or technical assistance to disadvantaged businesses or communities, or

children.label: B
children.text: which intends to satisfy the requirement under subsection (b)(1)(B) by making qualified low-income community investments in 1 or more businesses in which persons unrelated to such entity (within the meaning of section 267(b) or 707(b)(1)) hold the majority equity interest.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the new markets tax credit limitation for any calendar year exceeds the aggregate amount allocated under paragraph (2) for such year, such limitation for the succeeding calendar year shall be increased by the amount of such excess.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: No amount may be carried under subparagraph (A) to any calendar year afer the fifth calendar year after the calendar year in which the excess described in such subparagraph occurred. For purposes of this subparagraph, any excess described in subparagraph (A) with respect to any calendar year before 2026 shall be treated as occurring in calendar year 2025.

children.heading: Recapture of credit in certain cases
children.label: g Recapture of credit in certain cases

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If, at any time during the 7-year period beginning on the date of the original issue of a qualified equity investment in a qualified community development entity, there is a recapture event with respect to such investment, then the tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year in which such event occurs shall be increased by the credit recapture amount.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate decrease in the credits allowed to the taxpayer under section 38 for all prior taxable years which would have resulted if no credit had been determined under this section with respect to such investment, plus

children.label: B
children.text: interest at the underpayment rate established under section 6621 on the amount determined under subparagraph (A) for each prior taxable year for the period beginning on the due date for filing the return for the prior taxable year involved.

children.label: A
children.text: such entity ceases to be a qualified community development entity,

children.label: B
children.text: the proceeds of the investment cease to be used as required of subsection (b)(1)(B), or

children.label: C
children.text: such investment is redeemed by such entity.

children.heading: Tax benefit rule
children.label: A Tax benefit rule
children.text: The tax for the taxable year shall be increased under paragraph (1) only with respect to credits allowed by reason of this section which were used to reduce tax liability. In the case of credits not so used to reduce tax liability, the carryforwards and carrybacks under section 39 shall be appropriately adjusted.

children.heading: No credits against tax
children.label: B No credits against tax
children.text: Any increase in tax under this subsection shall not be treated as a tax imposed by this chapter for purposes of determining the amount of any credit under this chapter or for purposes of section 55.

children.heading: Basis reduction
children.label: h Basis reduction
children.text: The basis of any qualified equity investment shall be reduced by the amount of any credit determined under this section with respect to such investment. This subsection shall not apply for purposes of section 1202.

which limit the credit for investments which are directly or indirectly subsidized by other Federal tax benefits (including the credit under section 42 and the exclusion from gross income under section 103),
which prevent the abuse of the purposes of this section,
which provide rules for determining whether the requirement of subsection (b)(1)(B) is treated as met,
which impose appropriate reporting requirements,
which apply the provisions of this section to newly formed entities, and
which ensure that non-metropolitan counties receive a proportional allocation of qualified equity investments.
